fields.Identifying_Data_Chief_Complaint: 38-year-old Asian American female with schizoaffective disorder with symptom worsening.
fields.Subjective: Ms. Lee presents appearing anxious with husband providing collateral. Reports that medication costs doubled when insurance changed and she's been rationing her quetiapine, taking it only 3-4 times weekly instead of daily for past 5 weeks to make supply last. Since reducing frequency, experiencing significant worsening. Reports increased depression with anhedonia and difficulty getting out of bed most mornings. Paranoid thoughts intensified - believes neighbors can hear her thoughts and are reporting her to building management. Hearing whispers calling her name 4-5 times daily. Sleep severely disrupted at 3-5 hours. Appetite poor, weight down 7 lbs. Called out from nursing job 4 times this month, supervisor warning given. Lives with husband and children. Withdrawn from family activities. Mood 'anxious and hopeless' rated 3/10. Denies substance use. Safety: passive death wishes, denies plan, denies HI. (Documented by Lisa Chen, PMHNP-BC, Psychiatric Nurse Practitioner)

Mental_Status_Examination.Appearance: Appears tired, poor eye contact, tearful
Mental_Status_Examination.Thought_Content: Paranoid delusions about thought broadcasting, auditory hallucinations, passive death wishes
Mental_Status_Examination.Thought_Process: Slowed, preoccupied with paranoid and depressive themes

Behavioral_Health_Checkup.C_SSRS: Passive death wishes without plan or intent

Laboratory_Data.Recent_Lab_Results: Recent glucose 108 mg/dL, other labs normal.

fields.Diagnostic_Impression: Schizoaffective disorder, depressive type, with significant exacerbation due to cost-related medication rationing.
fields.Psychosocial_Context: Married with children; employed as nurse but job at risk; financial barriers to medication; passive death wishes.
Assessment_and_Plan: Severe symptom worsening due to medication rationing because of cost barriers.
Enrolled in pharmaceutical patient assistance program immediately.
Provided 30-day sample supply today to bridge gap.
Passive death wishes concerning - husband to monitor, crisis resources provided.
Referred to social work for financial counseling.
Work excuse provided, discussed FMLA if needed.
Urgent follow-up in 1 week to monitor safety and symptoms.
fields.Author: Note written by Lisa Chen, PMHNP-BC, Psychiatric Nurse Practitioner — Healthcare Workers Assistance Program, 3300 Hospital Drive, San Francisco, CA 94110, Phone: (555) 883-7721, Fax: (555) 883-7722
